headline: AI Maps Global Seagrass Meadows for First Time
meta_description: Scientists used artificial intelligence to create the first high-resolution map of seagrass meadows worldwide, revealing critical conservation gaps.
summary_short: Researchers have created the first detailed global map of seagrass meadows using AI, revealing where protection is most urgently needed.
body:
Seagrass meadows support extraordinary marine biodiversity and store carbon efficiently, yet they remain poorly understood and inadequately protected. Scientists at Arizona State University have now created the first high-resolution map of seagrass extent globally using satellite technology and artificial intelligence, revealing the scale and distribution of these vital ecosystems.

The mapping reveals that nearly seventy percent of the world's seagrass meadows are concentrated off the coasts of just five countries. However, only twenty-one percent of these meadows currently fall within marine protected areas—a significant gap in conservation coverage.

This breakthrough in mapping technology matters because it transforms seagrass from an overlooked ecosystem into something measurable and manageable. By showing exactly where seagrass meadows exist and where protection is lacking, the map provides a concrete foundation for conservation priorities. Countries and organizations can now target their efforts where they will have the greatest impact, turning data into action for marine ecosystems that sustain both wildlife and human communities.
